name: Crushed Carrots with Harissa and Pistachios

ingredients.item: olive oil, plus extra to finish
large carrots (2¼ lb/1 kg), peeled and cut into slices ¾-inch/2-cm thick scant 1 cup/200 ml vegetable stock grated zest of 1 orange
harissa paste grated zest of 1 lemon, plus 1 tbsp lemon juice
cup/200 g Greek yogurt scant 3½ tbsp/25 g shelled unsalted pistachios, coarsely chopped salt and black pepper

method: Place the olive oil and butter in a large sauté pan over medium-high heat. Add the carrots and sauté for 6 minutes, stirring often; they need to soften and take on a bit of color. Add the stock, turn down the heat to medium-low, cover the pan, and cook for another 25 minutes, until the carrots are completely soft and there is hardly any liquid left. Transfer the carrots to a
food processor, add ¾ teaspoon salt, and blitz briefly to form a coarse paste.
Leave to cool and then add the orange zest, garlic, harissa, half the lemon zest, and some black pepper. Stir to combine.
Mix together the yogurt, lemon juice, the remaining lemon zest, and ¼ teaspoon salt.
Spread the yogurt out on a serving platter and spoon the carrot mixture on top. Sprinkle with the pistachios, drizzle with a little olive oil, and serve.
